channel.title: Comments for page "The Escape From Site-132"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Escape From Site-132"

item.title: Series 3 tale
item.content:encoded:
I've added your tale to the Series 3 Tales under SCP-2150. Your tale involved that SCP and your tale is rated more then +10. All of it checked out, so I added it to the list.

Here's a link to get there.

item.title: Re: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
+ Show opinion post
- Hide click HERE to close

This one’s interesting, because it’s less an exploration into D-Class as a thing, and more of a D-Class’s adventure as a test subject. And it’s not the standard type of test subject, he’s basically set up to let it spiral out of control as far as it can possibly go.

And of course, the Foundation still has the strings in the end, or maybe better described as the leash, but he was still the catalyst.

I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Back at the beginning with no memories of what happened" might be better in a more neutral environment, but in the context of the contest, which I came into expecting to see a lot of, it falls flat as an ending.

The jumping around in time portion of the narrative, combined with memetic fuckery makes this extremely difficult to follow. In the end, it doesn't feel quite worth it enough to hang with that level of difficulty in exchange for a story about a guy taking over the Foundation for reasons that I'm still not quite clear on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, while I can appreciate the concept of a D-class using an anomaly they have tapped into to escape a Foundation site, this tale's dialogue is rather clunky, and the approach to the narrative comes across as kind of clunky. Additionally, the twist ending didn't really pan out for me, as I was predicting something along those lines from the get go, resulting in it falling a little flat.

-1.

item.title: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
This is my submission for the D-Class contest. And also my first contest submission, so I'm excited!

Thank you to Kate, and everyone else who reviewed my drafts (I can't remember the other names, but do let me know, and I'll put your name up here).

And similar to the article this tale is based off of, not everything is what appears to be on the surface.
